function_explanation: Converts input into a 1-dimensional read-only NumPy array of floats.
function_docstring:
Converts the input into a 1-dimensional read-only NumPy array of floats.

Args:
    x (Union[scalar, array-like]): A scalar or array-like object that can be converted into a 1-dimensional array.
        If x is a scalar, it will be converted into a single-element array.
        If x is an array-like object, it will be copied and converted into a 1-dimensional array.

Returns:
    np.ndarray: A 1-dimensional read-only NumPy array of floats with shape (n,), where n is the number of elements
    in the input. Raises a ValueError if the input cannot be reshaped into a 1-dimensional array.
usage_example:
import numpy as np

# Example usage of the vector function
scalar_input = 5
array_input = [1, 2, 3]

# Convert scalar to 1D array
scalar_vector = vector(scalar_input)
print(scalar_vector)  # Output: array([5.])

# Convert array-like input to 1D array
array_vector = vector(array_input)
print(array_vector)  # Output: array([1., 2., 3.])
